constraints_1_content: Before writing any processing script, use `list_dir` on `/app/data/in` to understand the actual directory structure, subdirectory nesting, and file naming patterns — do not assume only flat-level files exist.
constraints_1_rationale: Specific to exploring the input data directory of this reconciliation task; references the framework-specific list_dir tool.
constraints_2_content: Use `view_files` to inspect at least one sample row from an orders CSV and the first few lines of payments.jsonl / refunds.jsonl (if present) before writing any parsing logic, to confirm actual field names, quoting style, and timestamp formats in the real data.
constraints_2_rationale: Tied to the specific input formats of this task; uses the framework-specific view_files tool.
constraints_3_content: Use `run_command` with `find /app/data/in -type f | LC_ALL=C sort` to enumerate all input files before building the inventory, rather than hard-coding expected filenames beyond the wildcard patterns described.
constraints_3_rationale: Directly tied to generating inventory.csv for this task; references the framework run_command tool.
constraints_4_content: After generating each of the six output files, run a format check via `run_command`: verify no trailing spaces (`grep -Pn ' $' <file>` should produce no output), confirm LF-only line endings, and confirm exactly one trailing newline (`tail -c 1 <file> | xxd`).
constraints_4_rationale: Tied to the strict whitespace/newline output requirements of this task; uses run_command for machine-verifiable checks.
constraints_5_content: Never modify, delete, or truncate any file under `/app/data/in` during processing; treat the input directory as strictly read-only.
constraints_5_rationale: Safeguard specific to this file-based pipeline task where input corruption would silently break all downstream outputs; framework-independent safety rule.
constraints_6_content: Before calling `finish`, use `run_command` to cross-check that the counts embedded in summary.txt match the actual line counts of the other five output files (e.g., `wc -l /app/out/orders.csv` minus 1 should equal `total_orders_dedup`).
constraints_6_rationale: Tied to the derived-count semantics of summary.txt in this reconciliation task; references framework-specific run_command and finish tools.
